sanskrit_devanagari:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदु: ।
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतस: ॥ ३० ॥

anvaya.meaning: adhibhūta — and the governing principle of the material manifestation
cetasaḥ — their minds engaged in Me.

translations.verbatim_quote: Those in full consciousness of Me, who know Me, the Supreme Lord, to be the governing principle of the material manifestation, of the demigods, and of all methods of sacrifice, can understand and know Me, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, even at the time of death.

Those in full consciousness of Me, who know Me, the Supreme Lord, to be the governing principle of the material manifestation, of the demigods, and of all methods of sacrifice, can understand and know Me, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, even at the time of death.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2571 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Persons acting in Kṛṣṇa consciousness are never deviated from the path of entirely understanding the Supreme Personality of Godhead. In the transcendental association of Kṛṣṇa consciousness, one can understand how the Supreme Lord is the governing principle of the material manifestation and even of

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.30 Ye, those who; viduh, know; mam, Me; sa-adhi-bhuta-adhidaivam, as existing in the physical and the divine planes; ca, and also; sa-adhiyajnam, as existing in the context of the sacrifice; te, they; yukta-cetasah, of concentrated minds-those who have their minds absorbed in God; viduh,
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-30-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Those who know Krishna as existing in the physical and divine planes (sa-adhi-bhūta-adhidaivam) and also as existing in the context of sacrifice (sa-adhiyajñam) — they, with concentrated minds (yukta-cetasaḥ, those whose minds are absorbed in God), know Krishna even at the time of death (prayāṇa-kāle). Beyond the brahman-knowledge of 7.29, those devoted to God hold knowledge of all individuals, all actions, and the worship-context.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.30 Here, other qualified persons distinct from those already mentioned (i.e., those who desire Kaivalya) are to be understood, because of the mention again of the term ‘those’ (ye).
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-30-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Here, qualified persons distinct from those mentioned in 7.29 are described — those who know Krishna as connected with adhibhūta (higher material entities) and adhidaiva (the self in its lordship) and adhiyajña (the sacrifice-context). The 7.30 attainment is higher than 7.29's kaivalya; the prayāṇa-kāle clause sets up Ch 8's death-time doctrine.

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Shankara and Ramanuja both treat 7.30 as forward-pointing to Ch 8's prayāṇa-kāla doctrine — the question of how Krishna is known at the time of departure. Shankara reads the verse as completing 7.29's brahman-knowledge by adding the action-context (adhiyajña) and the elemental-and-divine-context (adhibhūta-adhidaiva); Ramanuja reads it as a separate, higher attainment than 7.29's kaivalya. The engineering analog is heavily STRETCHED and explicitly forward-points to Ch 8. The chapter closes with a metaphysical reach the engineering layer cannot match.

literal_meaning: Those who know me in adhibhūta, adhidaiva, adhiyajña; even at the time of departure, they know me, of yoked mind.
traditional_meaning_consensus: sādhibhūtādhidaivaṁ māṁ sādhiyajñaṁ ca ye viduḥ / prayāṇa-kāle 'pi ca māṁ te vidur yukta-cetasaḥ — those who know Krishna together with adhibhūta (the basis of the elements / the higher material entities), adhidaiva (the basis of the divinities / that which is higher to divinities), and adhiyajña (the basis of sacrifice / that which is in the context of sacrifice) — they, of yoked mind (yukta-cetasaḥ), know Krishna even at the time of departure (prayāṇa-kāle). Shankara: those who know Krishna in his three further aspects, with mind absorbed (yukta-cetasaḥ), know him even at death — beyond the brahman-knowledge of 7.29, they know the action-context and the worship-context as Krishna. Ramanuja: this verse names a higher attainment than 7.29's kaivalya — those who know Krishna as connected with adhibhūta and adhidaiva, with the goal of becoming refuged in the supreme Lord; the prayāṇa-kāle clause sets up Ch 8's prayāṇa-kāla doctrine.

remaining_gaps: Madhva, Abhinavagupta, Sridhara Swami, Kesava Kashmiri commentaries available in raw/ but not pulled into the source pack.
